proposition.text: The Defendant knew, or ought to have known, of bugs, errors and defects in the Horizon system that could cause discrepancies in branch accounts.

links.rationale: The CEO admits she knew Horizon had bugs because she had been told, directly supporting Defendant's knowledge.

links.rationale: Confirms a judicial finding that Horizon bugs could cause apparent discrepancies in branch accounts, supporting the existence of defects capable of causing discrepancies.

links.quote: I was not aware of the failure rates detailed in the report nor the fact that these errors could cause discrepancies in branch accounts

links.rationale: Personal ignorance of a bugs list, but its content confirms such a list existed within the organisation, leaving net effect ambiguous.
links.quote: I had no idea during my period of employment with POL that a list or lists of bugs/errors and defects with the Horizon system existed.

links.rationale: Asserts the errors it handled would not cause branch account discrepancies, undermining the claim of discrepancy-causing defects.
links.quote: These were non-financial data input errors which would not cause the branch account to have any discrepancies

links.rationale: Denies any involvement in bug-related issues, providing no probative content either way.
links.quote: I never had any involvement in "issues relating to bugs, errors and defects in Horizon"

links.rationale: Confirms Fujitsu held a list of bugs but the witness only learned of it late, leaving the effect ambiguous.
links.quote: It was only on reading the list of issues for this inquiry that I became aware that Fujitsu held a list or lists of bugs/errors and defects in Horizon.

links.rationale: Denies awareness or experience of any bugs, errors or defects, supporting a denial of knowledge.
links.quote: I have not been aware of or witnessed any bugs, errors or defects in the Horizon system whilst performing my daily duties

links.rationale: Describes an internal reporting procedure for bugs affecting prosecutions without confirming actual knowledge of defects.
links.quote: I believe this would have applied to any Bugs errors or defects in the Horizon system that could have had an impact on a prosecution

links.rationale: Denies awareness of any bugs, errors or defects, supporting a denial of knowledge.
links.quote: I was not aware of any bugs, errors, defects or problems with the Horizon system at any point throughout my involvement in this prosecution

links.rationale: Denies awareness of bugs while working for POL, supporting a denial of knowledge.
links.quote: Whilst working for POL I was unaware that there were bugs, errors or defects in Horizon.

links.rationale: Denies awareness of bugs or integrity concerns, supporting a denial of knowledge.
links.quote: I have been asked if I was aware of any bugs, errors or defects or concerns with the integrity of Horizon or complaints about the same. I was not

links.rationale: Denies awareness of bugs as a HFSO and during training, supporting a denial of knowledge.
links.quote: I was not aware of any bugs, errors or defects within the Horizon IT system at the time I was a HSFO nor was I made aware of any issues of that type during my training

links.rationale: States no information about known bugs was provided, tending to support a denial of knowledge.
links.quote: 1 received no information by anyone from Fujitsu (or the Post Office) during the course of the criminal proceedings against Grant Allen with regard to known bugs, errors or defects in the Horizon IT system

links.rationale: Asserts no information about known bugs was provided, tending to support a denial of knowledge.
links.quote: 1 received no information by anyone from Fujitsu (or the Post Office) during the course of the criminal proceedings against Angela Sefton and Anne Nield with regard to known bugs, errors or defects in the Horizon IT system

links.rationale: States no knowledge of bugs before a certain date, supporting a denial of knowledge though implying later awareness.
links.quote: 1 had no knowledge of any bugs, errors, or defects in the Horizon system prior to 27 June 2013

links.rationale: States no information regarding bugs was provided, supporting a denial of knowledge.
links.quote: No information was given to me regarding bugs, errors and defects in Horizon

links.rationale: States no information about known bugs was given, supporting a denial of knowledge.
links.quote: At no point was any information given to me by anyone from Fujitsu (or the Post Office) during the course of the criminal proceedings against Angela Sefton and Anne Nield about known bugs, errors or defects

links.rationale: States no information regarding bugs was ever given, supporting a denial of knowledge.
links.quote: I was never given any information regarding bugs, errors and defects in Horizon.

links.rationale: Denies any awareness of bugs, supporting a denial of knowledge.
links.quote: At no time was I aware of any Horizon, bugs, errors or defects so I would not have disclosed anything relating to that.

links.rationale: Does not recall being made aware of bugs, supporting a denial of knowledge.
links.quote: I do not recall ever being made aware of any bugs, errors or defects in the system whilst I was an Auditor

links.rationale: States he was not informed of bugs that may have caused losses, supporting a denial of actual knowledge.
links.quote: I do not recollect being informed by POL or Fujitsu of such issues.

proposition.text: The Defendant had the ability to access and alter branch account data remotely without the knowledge or consent of the subpostmaster.
proposition.quote: The Defendant had the ability to access and alter branch account data remotely without the knowledge or consent of the subpostmaster

links.rationale: Confirms transactions could be injected into branch accounts without the subpostmaster's knowledge or consent.
links.quote: Mr. Clarke noted that it was possible to `inject' a transaction unilaterally into the accounting records of a branch without the consent, approval or indeed the knowledge of the Subpostmaster

links.rationale: Judicial finding that transaction data could be edited by Fujitsu without PMRs' knowledge supports the proposition.
links.quote: the true position on remote access became known, namely that .transaction data can be edited by Fujitsu without the knowledge of PMRs

links.rationale: Confirms it was possible to inject a transaction into branch accounts without the SPMR's knowledge or consent.
links.quote: it was possible to 'inject' a transaction unilaterally into a branch's accounting records without the consent, approval or indeed knowledge of the SPMR.

links.rationale: Confirms Fujitsu could actually put an entry into a branch account remotely.
links.quote: found out that Fujitsu can actually put an entry into a branch account remotely.

links.rationale: States it was theoretically possible for privileged Fujitsu administrators to alter transactions remotely.
links.quote: it was theoretically possible for administrators at Fujitsu with the appropriate level of privilege to alter transactions.

links.rationale: Directly asserts data could not be changed remotely without SPM knowledge, contradicting the proposition.
links.quote: POL and Fujitsu maintained the position that transaction data could not be changed remotely without the knowledge of an SPM

links.rationale: States it was not possible to alter branch records remotely without SPMs' knowledge, contradicting the proposition.
links.quote: I had always been given the same answer: that it was not possible for branch records to be altered remotely without the SPMs knowledge

links.rationale: Acknowledges Fujitsu could technically access a branch remotely and move money around, supporting the capability.
links.quote: Technically, Fujitsu could access an individual branch remotely and move money around however this has never happened yet

links.rationale: Accepts that Fujitsu could put an entry into a branch account remotely via Horizon.
links.quote: I was not aware that Fujitsu could put an entry into a branch account remotely via Horizon, but accept comments in the email text that this was possible

links.rationale: Asserts nobody in POL could remotely access the in-branch Horizon system, contradicting the proposition.
links.quote: During the interview I stated that nobody in POL could remotely access the in-branch Horizon system because it is all done from a user ID.

links.rationale: States there was no back door for POL or Fujitsu to alter figures without SPMs' knowledge.
links.quote: We were assured that there was no "back door" into the system for Post Office or Fujitsu to alter the figures unbeknownst to the Sub Postmasters (SPMs).

links.rationale: Confirms SSC staff had access to live branch data from a remote secure location.
links.quote: SSC staff members with access to the SSC PCs had access to Horizon, including live Post Office branch data, from the secure area in Bracknell.

links.rationale: Reflects POL's understanding that branch data could not be accessed and amended without SPM knowledge.
links.quote: it is not, and never has been, possible for anyone to access Branch Data and

links.rationale: States global access users still had to physically be in front of the terminal, undercutting remote access.
links.quote: The global access user would still need to physically be in front of the Horizon terminal

links.rationale: Concerns subpostmaster access after suspension, not the defendant's remote alteration capability.
links.quote: I do not think the subpostmaster would be able to access Horizon data after the suspension

links.rationale: Concerns audit needing in-branch access, not remote alteration capability.
links.quote: this confirmed my recollection that a full audit could not be completed without access to the Horizon system in branch

links.rationale: Describes error mitigations, not the defendant's remote access or alteration capability.
links.quote: mitigations against this included balancing routines in branch, batch controls in data transfers and the ability to request evidence to query the transaction corrections.

evidence_source.doc_id: WITN04630100 - Rod Ismay - First Witness Statement

links.rationale: Describes discrepancy checking and finding none, not the defendant's capability to remotely alter data.
links.quote: Mrs Chambers checked for discrepancies between these and found none.

links.rationale: This concerns the witness's lack of knowledge of such ability, not whether the ability itself existed.
links.quote: Pre-2011, 1 had no knowledge of the ability of Fujitsu employees to alter transaction data or data in branch accounts without the knowledge or consent of SPMs.

links.rationale: Addresses the witness's recollection of personal knowledge, not the existence of the capability.
links.quote: I do not recall having this knowledge at any point during my time at POL.

links.rationale: Concerns the witness's awareness, not whether the alteration capability existed.
links.quote: I do not recall being aware of Fujitsu's use of access rights and privileges to alter transaction data or data in branch accounts

links.rationale: Speaks to the witness's lack of concern, not to whether the capability existed.
links.quote: I was never concerned that Fujitsu might be adjusting transaction data without the knowledge of SPMs

links.rationale: Affirms theoretical remote access but qualifies that it could not be done without SPMs' knowledge.
links.quote: Fujitsu employees could, in theory, have remote access but not without the knowledge of SPMs.

links.rationale: Concerns NBSC's lack of access to data, not the defendant's remote alteration capability.
links.quote: I do think that NBSC did what they could given that they did not have access to any data apart from that provided by the branch

links.rationale: Describes auditor global accounts with managerial access but does not establish covert remote alteration of branch data.
links.quote: they would create a Global User Account for every auditor. The Global User Account was a unique account which allowed an auditor to log on to any branch with managerial level access

proposition.text: The Defendant required subpostmasters to make good apparent shortfalls out of their own funds, notwithstanding that the shortfalls were caused by the Horizon system.
proposition.quote: The Defendant required subpostmasters to make good apparent shortfalls out of their own funds, notwithstanding that the shortfalls were caused by the Horizon system

links.rationale: Confirms subpostmasters were asked to make good shortfalls at audit as a contractual obligation.
links.quote: I was aware of the process of asking subpostmasters to make good any shortfalls at the time of audit. I believe this was the subpostmasters' contractual obligation.

links.rationale: States subpostmasters were contractually liable for shortfalls which had to be made good.
links.quote: In contractual terms, they were liable, contractually, for any shortfalls which had to be made good.

links.rationale: Confirms agency branches were liable to make good any and all shortfalls.
links.quote: contractually, agency branches were accountable for the cash and transactions in their branch and liable to make good any and all shortfalls

links.rationale: States subpostmasters had to make good all losses and would have to pay absent disputing evidence, supporting the requirement.
links.quote: My understanding is that Subpostmasters had to make good all losses under the terms of their contract and if they didn't have any evidence to dispute the error they would need to make it good.

links.rationale: Directly states subpostmasters were required to make good unresolved shortfalls.
links.quote: If further investigation did not resolve the shortfall the subpostmaster was required to make good the shortfall.

links.rationale: States Sub Post Office owners were contractually required to make good any shortfall.
links.quote: In Sub Post Office the owners were required under their contract to make good any shortfall.

links.rationale: Judicial finding that Horizon bugs materially risked causing shortfalls supports the causation element.
links.quote: Mr Justice Fraser concluded that there was a material risk that bugs in older versions of Horizon had caused shortfalls in branches.

links.rationale: Confirms subpostmasters were requested to make good shortages at audit.
links.quote: Any difference would represent the Branch surplus or shortage which Subpostmasters would be requested to withdraw or make good which would be undertaken at the time of the audit.

links.rationale: Shows processes assumed Horizon could not cause shortfalls, supporting that subpostmasters were made to pay regardless of Horizon errors.
links.quote: The processes followed and improvements that I identified were based on the assumption that the Horizon system could not cause discrepancies and cash shortfalls.

links.rationale: Pleads that apparent shortfalls were entirely the product of Horizon problems, supporting the causation element.
links.quote:
The Defendant avers that any apparent shortfall is entirely the
product of problems with the Horizon computer and accounting system used by the Claimant.

links.rationale: Witness attributes the shortfalls to the Horizon system, supporting the causation element.
links.quote: As the shortfalls began happening on such a regular basis it could only be the Horizon system in our opinion.

links.rationale: Concerns the witness's lack of involvement in other proceedings, not the requirement or causation.
links.quote: I have not been involved in any other proceedings against SPMs, their managers, assistants or Post Office employees involving shortfalls shown by the Horizon IT system before this case.

links.rationale: Concerns lack of involvement in other proceedings, not relevant to the requirement or causation.
links.quote: I have not been involved in any other proceedings against SPMs, their managers or assistants, or Post Office employees involving shortfalls shown by the Horizon IT system

links.rationale: Concerns lack of involvement in prior proceedings, not the requirement or causation.
links.quote: To the best of my knowledge I had not been involved in any other proceedings against sub-postmasters, their managers or assistants or Post Office employees involving shortfalls shown by the Horizon IT system before this case

links.rationale: POL's denial that Horizon generated false shortfalls contradicts the causation element of the proposition.
links.quote: As we did not believe that the Horizon IT System generated any false shortfalls, we did not take any particular steps in this regard.

links.rationale: Concerns who made prosecution decisions, not the requirement to make good shortfalls.
links.quote: Prosecution and charging decisions in respect of SPMs, managers, assistants and Crown Office employees alleged to be responsible for shortfalls shown by data from the Horizon IT system were made by the lawyer who had conduct of the case.

links.rationale: Concerns reporting thresholds for shortfalls, not whether subpostmasters had to pay them.
links.quote: My recollection is that shortfalls over £1,000 had to be reported.

links.rationale: Frames liability as limited to losses caused by the subpostmaster's own negligence or error, not Horizon-caused shortfalls.
links.quote: The Contract provided that he was obliged to make good all losses caused through his own negligence, carelessness or error and losses of any kind caused by his assistants.

links.rationale: Concerns whether the witness changed his view of Horizon, not the requirement to make good shortfalls.
links.quote: I do not believe that I changed my view about Horizon and shortfalls as a result of this review.

evidence_source.doc_id: WITN04630100 - Rod Ismay - First Witness Statement

links.rationale: Addresses absence of future plans for shortfall management processes, not whether subpostmasters were required to pay.
links.quote: there was simply no plan to improve the processes that sit around the system, including the management of shortfalls, the data needed to support them or for the roll-out of a new IT system.

links.rationale: Concerns Crown Office employees' non-liability, not subpostmasters, so it doesn't directly move the proposition about subpostmasters.
links.quote: employees working in Crown Post Offices were not required to make good shortfalls in their till, and that they were not personally liable for such shortfalls.

links.rationale: Limits responsibility to fault-based shortfalls rather than Horizon-caused ones, undermining the 'notwithstanding Horizon' claim.
links.quote: the subpostmaster was responsible for shortfalls or losses incurred at the branch caused through his/her negligence, carelessness or error

links.rationale: Denies awareness of any practice of auditors asking subpostmasters to make good discrepancies, undermining the requirement claim.
links.quote: I was not aware of any practice where auditors asked subpostmasters to make good discrepancies on the day of audit.

links.rationale: Asserts auditors would consider Horizon issues as causes, undermining the 'notwithstanding Horizon' aspect.
links.quote: Auditors in my team would not have been instructed to ignore Horizon issues if they were raised as possible reasons for shortfalls.

links.rationale: Describes assurances of Horizon reliability affecting data requests, but does not directly address the requirement to make good shortfalls.
links.quote: I cannot recall if data would be requested in all cases of cash shortfalls as we had been assured by Fujitsu that the Horizon system was completely reliable.

proposition.text: The Defendant pursued recovery of, and in some cases prosecuted subpostmasters for, shortfalls that were the product of Horizon errors rather than dishonesty or negligence.
proposition.quote: The Defendant pursued recovery of, and in some cases prosecuted subpostmasters for, shortfalls that were the product of Horizon errors rather than dishonesty or negligence

links.rationale: About a product role, unrelated to the proposition.
links.quote: I was the product owner of Smart ID for the duration of the programme

links.rationale: Concerns the scope of prosecution data, not the cause of shortfalls.
links.quote: This data is limited to cases prosecuted by POL in England and Wales

links.rationale: A pleaded defence asserting the shortfall was entirely the product of Horizon problems aligns with the proposition.
links.quote:
any apparent shortfall is entirely the
product of problems with the Horizon computer and accounting system

links.rationale: Concerns rollout timing, irrelevant to shortfall causation or recovery.
links.quote: a pause in rolling out Horizon further following concerns raised by subpostmasters

links.rationale: Administrative collation of cases for non-POL prosecutors, not bearing on causation.
links.quote: collated into separate lists and sent to those non-POL prosecutors

links.rationale: Reveals charging strategy to avoid Horizon being questioned, supporting that prosecutions masked Horizon issues.
links.quote: charged with false accounting rather than theft and false accounting on the proviso that the horizon system is not brought into question

links.rationale: Indicates a prosecution proceeded without Horizon being investigated for faults, consistent with pursuing Horizon-related shortfalls.
links.quote: No indication in file that Horizon was investigated for faults / errors

links.rationale: Attributes losses to subpostmaster negligence/error and contractual liability, opposing the Horizon-error explanation.
links.quote: sub-postmasters were contractually liable for losses (e.g. through negligence, carelessness, innocent errors

links.rationale: Asserts no prosecution was stopped due to Horizon errors, contradicting that shortfalls were Horizon-caused.
links.quote: none was stopped because of errors found in the Horizon system

links.rationale: General view of subpostmaster honesty, not tied to the cause of shortfalls.
links.quote: Some of the subpostmasters GRo weren't up to the job and some were dishonest, but most were honest

links.rationale: Describes a process to check for errors warranting transaction corrections, not establishing recovery for Horizon errors.
links.quote: to see if any errors had occurred for which a Transaction Correction was due

links.rationale: Describes a Fujitsu reporting process to resolve issues, neutral on the proposition.
links.quote: reports could be requested from Fujitsu in some cases to help resolve issues

links.rationale: Shows prosecutions relied on Horizon data for discrepancies, linking pursuit to Horizon figures.
links.quote: the evidence of discrepancies was provided by the Horizon data

links.rationale: Lack of clear policy on referral does not address whether shortfalls were Horizon errors.
links.quote: I was not aware of any clear policy why certain cases were referred for civil debt recovery.

links.rationale: Frames shortfalls as caused by the subpostmaster's own negligence or error, undercutting the claim they were Horizon errors.
links.quote: the subpostmaster was responsible for shortfalls or losses incurred at the branch caused through his/her negligence, carelessness or error

links.rationale: Confirms the practice of referring cases to civil litigation for debt recovery, supporting the recovery element.
links.quote: then the case would sometimes be referred to civil litigation for debt recovery.

links.rationale: Downplays early Horizon issues as not affecting accounting, undercutting Horizon-error causation.
links.quote: I don't believe that these impacted the overall accounting at the end of the periods provided recovery was carried out correctly

links.rationale: References a period when they prosecuted Horizon cases before stopping, implying Horizon-based prosecutions occurred.
links.quote: after we stopped prosecuting the Horizon cases as that case did not rely upon Horizon evidence

links.rationale: Concerns disclosure of privileged material to Second Sight, not the cause of shortfalls.
links.quote: the legally privileged material that POL would not make available to the defendant or Court would not be provided to Second Sight.

links.rationale: Suggests pursued cases were genuine and well-founded rather than Horizon artefacts.
links.quote: Ail cases pursued on this basis were found in favour of the Post Office

evidence_source.doc_id: WITN05290200 - David Smith - Second Witness Statement

links.rationale: Acknowledges Horizon errors existed but minimises their scope, not clearly moving the proposition.
links.quote: my understanding was that Horizon had some errors, but these were few

links.rationale: Shows recovery of shortfalls was demanded but characterises it as contractual, not addressing cause.
links.quote: the process of asking subpostmasters to make good any shortfalls at the time of audit

links.rationale: Frames shortfalls as a contractual liability of the subpostmaster, not as Horizon errors.
links.quote: they were liable, contractually, for any shortfalls which had to be made good.

links.rationale: Confirms prosecutions were brought on behalf of the Post Office, the prosecution element.
links.quote: I prosecuted cases on behalf of the Post Office and Royal Mail.

proposition.text: The Defendant failed to investigate adequately reports by subpostmasters of unexplained discrepancies and system faults.
proposition.quote: The Defendant failed to investigate adequately reports by subpostmasters of unexplained discrepancies and system faults

links.rationale: Concerns adequacy of training time, not investigation of reported discrepancies or faults.
links.quote: In my view, there was enough time provided to train SPMs to adequately use the Horizon IT system

links.rationale: Notes no indication Horizon was investigated for faults/errors in the file, supporting failure to investigate.
links.quote: No indication in file that Horizon was investigated for faults / errors

links.rationale: Indicates it was almost impossible to challenge or investigate Horizon information, supporting inadequate investigation.
links.quote: it would have been almost impossible for solicitors to challenge the information they received

links.rationale: Concerns training adequacy, not investigation of fault reports.
links.quote: some sub postmasters were adequately trained in how to use Horizon and others may not have been.

links.rationale: Criticises an investigation report as too caveated and inconclusive, supporting inadequate investigation.
links.quote: Deloitte's report was far too caveated and failed to reach any definitive conclusions on the reliability of the Horizon IT System

links.rationale: Concerns business case risk assessment, unrelated to investigating subpostmaster fault reports.
links.quote: BA's initial business case did not adequately assess the risk and costs of serious slippage.

links.rationale: Mere awareness of litigation claims; does not address whether reports were adequately investigated.
links.quote: I did become aware of some sub-postmasters taking legal action against POL

links.rationale: Concerns audit-day make-good practices, not investigation of fault reports.
links.quote: I was not aware of any practice where auditors asked subpostmasters to make good discrepancies on the day of audit.

links.rationale: Concerns design of the suspense account and blames subpostmasters, not investigation of fault reports.
links.quote: historically it was used by Subpostmasters to" hide" discrepancies in their accounts

links.rationale: Describes generating reports for audits, not investigation of reported faults or discrepancies.
links.quote: I would have used my access to the Horizon system to generate the reports required to undertake the audit.

links.rationale: States no evidence of faults was found, suggesting investigation occurred and found nothing rather than a failure to investigate.
links.quote: we had found no evidence of faults with the Horizon system

links.rationale: Confirms bugs/errors could cause discrepancies, supporting that subpostmaster reports warranted proper investigation.
links.quote: bugs, errors or defects in the Horizon system to have the potential to cause apparent discrepancies

links.rationale: Suggests faults were rare and he was unaware of any, undermining the premise that there were reports needing investigation.
links.quote: Yes, it would correspond to my opinion, as I was unaware of any faults.

links.rationale: Supports the view that faults were very rare, undermining the claim of unaddressed fault reports.
links.quote: the suggestion that system faults with Horizon were "very rare" corresponded with my understanding of the position in 2003.

links.rationale: Documents a subpostmaster's report of unexplained losses blamed on Horizon but does not address investigation adequacy.
links.quote: Mr Castleton was experiencing unexplained losses. I told her that that Mr Castleton thought that the Horizon system was to blame

links.rationale: Endorses that system faults were very rare, undermining the premise of reports requiring investigation.
links.quote: "System faults are very rare..." was exactly my understanding of the situation

links.rationale: Describes assisting subpostmasters to investigate and resolve unknown errors, indicating investigation did occur.
links.quote: investigate and try to resolve any unknown errors and to ensure they made good losses.

links.rationale: States the board was informed of faults and rectification steps, suggesting faults were addressed rather than ignored.
links.quote: the POL board was informed of any major outages or faults in the Horizon IT System, and the steps taken to rectify them

links.rationale: Records a subpostmaster alleging a Horizon error but does not address the adequacy of investigation.
links.quote: there is an error with Horizon

links.rationale: Shows a review into the system and prosecution claims was undertaken with a QC, indicating investigation was conducted.
links.quote: Tim Parker agreed... he would undertake a review into the Post Office `Horizon' IT system and various claims

links.rationale: Asserts the data integrity issue was adequately understood, undermining the claim of failure to investigate.
links.quote: I consider Pathway adequately understood this data integrity issue as it presented itself.

links.rationale: Describes engagement in mediation generally rather than investigation of specific fault reports.
links.quote: the Post Office has a willingness to explore the options, express empathy

links.rationale: Describes a routing mechanism for faults but does not address adequacy of investigating subpostmaster reports.
links.quote: apart from `system faults' `failures' or the `system not performing as expected' which would be redirected to the HSH

links.rationale: Records an opinion that calls did not affect integrity, indicating some review but not addressing adequacy of investigating subpostmaster reports.
links.quote: none of the calls related to faults which would have affected the of the information held on the system

links.rationale: Describes Fujitsu-identified incident reports for investigation, not subpostmaster reports or their adequacy.
links.quote: Fujitsu identified system issues in Horizon that required P&BA investigation

proposition.text: The Defendant failed to disclose to subpostmasters the existence of known faults in the Horizon system.
proposition.quote: The Defendant failed to disclose to subpostmasters the existence of known faults in the Horizon system

links.rationale: Directly evidences that a key witness knew of Horizon matters and failed to disclose them.
links.quote: Gareth Jenkins knew of the matters that were to be mentioned in the Second Sight Interim Report of 81h July 2013 and had failed to disclose them

links.rationale: Merely names a new financial system and is unrelated to disclosure of Horizon faults.
links.quote: system to be known as the Post Office Limited Financials System (POL-FS)

evidence_source.doc_id: WITN04790100 - Philip Boardman - First Witness Statement

links.rationale: Shows POL actively preparing to disclose a Horizon bug to affected branches, which contradicts a blanket failure to disclose.
links.quote: POL, with assistance from Bond Dickinson, was preparing letters to 14 affected branches to disclose the existence of a Horizon bug

links.rationale: Concerns disclosure of conviction evidence to police, not disclosure of Horizon faults to subpostmasters.
links.quote: he should immediately disclose this to the police.

links.rationale: Asserts no evidence of faults was found, undermining the premise that known faults existed to disclose.
links.quote: As at that date we had found no evidence of faults with the Horizon system or unsafe r

links.rationale: Shows that despite known claims, POL messaged that the system could not impact accounts, consistent with non-disclosure of faults.
links.quote: the messaging coming from the business was that the system was not capable of impacting the accounts.

links.rationale: Concerns whether Horizon was investigated for faults in one file, not whether known faults were disclosed to subpostmasters.
links.quote: No indication in file that Horizon was investigated for faults / errors

links.rationale: Supports a belief that faults were rare, undercutting that there were known faults being concealed.
links.quote: the suggestion that system faults with Horizon were "very rare" corresponded with my understanding of the position in 2003.

links.rationale: Witness states he was unaware of any faults, supporting a position that faults were not known.
links.quote: Yes, it would correspond to my opinion, as I was unaware of any faults.

links.rationale: Supports a contemporaneous understanding that faults were very rare, undercutting that known faults were withheld.
links.quote: "System faults are very rare..." was exactly my understanding of the situation with the Horizon system at that time and throughout

links.rationale: Describes call-routing procedure for faults, not disclosure of known faults to subpostmasters.
links.quote: apart from `system faults' `failures' or the `system not performing as expected' which would be redirected to the HSH

links.rationale: Describes commissioning of a review into Horizon claims, not non-disclosure of faults.
links.quote: Tim Parker agreed with Baroness Neville-Rolfe that he would undertake a review into the Post Office `Horizon' IT system

links.rationale: The existence of a Known Error Log shows errors on Horizon were internally recorded and known.
links.quote: The purpose of the KEL system was to provide information about errors reported on Horizon.

links.rationale: States a subpostmaster's defence that shortfalls stemmed from Horizon problems, not whether the Defendant disclosed faults.
links.quote:
The Defendant avers that any apparent shortfall is entirely the
product of problems with the Horizon computer and accounting system used by the Claimant.

links.rationale: States no specific faults were found in spot reviews, undercutting that known faults were concealed.
links.quote: [n]one of the Spot Reviews to date have found specific faults with Horizon

links.rationale: Concerns a litigation disclosure/inspection request, not the Defendant's disclosure of known faults to subpostmasters.
links.quote: a request for access to the Horizon computer system used by the defendant for inspection

links.rationale: Concerns the inconclusiveness of Deloitte's report, not non-disclosure of known faults to subpostmasters.
links.quote: I think that Deloitte's report was far too caveated and failed to reach any definitive conclusions on the reliability of the Horizon IT System

links.rationale: Shows the existence of bugs and errors was not disclosed even to the supervising department, consistent with a non-disclosure pattern.
links.quote: neither I nor the Board of BIS were informed about, or otherwise had knowledge of, the existence of bugs and errors in the Horizon IT system

links.rationale: Opinion that none of the calls related to integrity-affecting faults undercuts the claim of known concealed faults.
links.quote: It was his opinion that none of the calls related to faults which would have affected the of the information held on the system

links.rationale: Records a defendant's allegation of a Horizon error, not whether the Defendant disclosed known faults.
links.quote: there is an error with Horizon

links.rationale: POL communicated no evidence of faults had been found, asserting absence rather than concealment of faults.
links.quote: we have found no evidence, nor has any been advanced by either an Applicant or Second Sight of either faults with the Horizon system or unsafe convictions

links.rationale: POL was confidently communicating no evidence of faults, contrary to the claim that known faults were withheld.

links.rationale: Concerns internal resource requests, unrelated to representations about reliability to subpostmasters.
links.quote: I do not recall any occasions where I denied requests for additional resources

links.rationale: Describes audit criteria, irrelevant to whether Horizon's reliability was misrepresented.
links.quote: Audits would be denied if: • They were requested by an agent.

links.rationale: Witness's lack of awareness of allegations does not address whether reliability was misrepresented.
links.quote: At no point was I aware of any allegations made by Ms Adedayo relating to the reliability of the Horizon IT system.

links.rationale: Witness's lack of supplied reliability information does not address whether the Defendant misrepresented reliability.
links.quote: I was not supplied with any information in relation to the Horizon system

links.rationale: A disclosure/inspection application is procedural and does not bear on misrepresentation of reliability.
links.quote: a request for access to the Horizon computer system used by the defendant for inspection

links.rationale: A theft admission in a separate case does not bear on misrepresentation of reliability.
links.quote: The defendant admitted stealing money from the Post Office but disputed quantum.

links.rationale: Describes a defendant's allegation of a Horizon error, not any representation by the Defendant about reliability.
links.quote: the functionality of the Horizon System would be an issue

links.rationale: Lack of recollection of reliability allegations does not address misrepresentation.
links.quote: I do not recall of being made aware of any allegations at that time made by Ms Misra relating to the reliability of Horizon

links.rationale: Notes the rarity of reliability being raised in particular trials, not relevant to any representation made to subpostmasters.
links.quote: the only occasion I have found that Horizon's reliability was raised as any kind of issue

links.rationale: Witness first becoming aware of reliability issues does not address whether the Defendant misrepresented reliability.
links.quote: This was the first time in the case that I was made aware of the issue of Horizon IT reliability

links.rationale: A definitional reference to 'Horizon cases' does not move the misrepresentation question.
links.quote: cases where the reliability of Horizon evidence was essential to the prosecution

links.rationale: Describes subpostmasters blaming Horizon once reliability was questioned, not any representation by the Defendant.
links.quote: once the reliability of the system began to be questioned I recall that there were more cases where Horizon was blamed for the loss

links.rationale: A pleaded averment that shortfalls stemmed from Horizon problems is the opposing party's case, not proof of misrepresentation.
links.quote:
any apparent shortfall is entirely the
product of problems with the Horizon computer and accounting system

links.rationale: Lack of recollection of allegations does not bear on whether reliability was misrepresented.
links.quote: I do not recall being made aware of any allegations

links.rationale: Appointment of independent auditors to examine reliability is procedural and does not establish misrepresentation.
links.quote: independent auditors by the name of Second Sight had been appointed to report on the reliability of Horizon Online

links.rationale: Concerns an employee denying a manual-working arrangement under oath, tangential to misrepresenting Horizon's reliability.
links.quote: Catherine agreed to this but later denied it under oath.

links.rationale: Criticism of Deloitte's caveated report addresses reliability uncertainty but not any misrepresentation to subpostmasters.
links.quote: failed to reach any definitive conclusions on the reliability of the Horizon IT System

links.rationale: Shows reliability of Horizon was internally flagged as an issue to POL, tending to undermine the denial of misrepresentation.
links.quote: highlighted particular issues to POL during this exercise including the reliability of Horizon

links.rationale: An internal report on concerns about Horizon's reliability suggests POL was aware of reliability concerns, undermining the denial.
links.quote: a report authored by Rod Ismay, Head of P&BA dated 2 August 2010 in relation to concerns about the reliability of Horizon

links.rationale: Lack of awareness of widespread reliability concerns tends to support the denial of misrepresentation, though the quote is garbled.
links.quote: was not in a position to manage any information in respect of it

links.rationale: POL disclosing reliability issues shows issues existed but also transparency, leaving the misrepresentation question balanced.
links.quote: there were significant issues coming to light with the reliability of the 'Horizon' IT platform

proposition.text: Under the terms of their contracts the subpostmasters were responsible for losses arising in their branches.
proposition.quote: under the terms of their contracts the subpostmasters were responsible for losses arising in their branches

links.rationale: Directly states subpostmasters had to make good losses under their contract.
links.quote: My understanding is that Subpostmasters had to make good all losses under the terms of their contract

links.rationale: Directly states under the contract the subpostmaster was responsible for all losses.
links.quote: Under the contract the Sub Postmaster was responsible for all losses including those incurred by his staff.

links.rationale: States the contractual obligation was that subpostmasters were responsible for making good losses.
links.quote: they were responsible for making good any losses incurred. This was always defined in their contract

links.rationale: States subpostmasters were contractually liable for shortfalls which had to be made good.
links.quote: In contractual terms, they were liable, contractually, for any shortfalls which had to be made good.

links.rationale: States subpostmasters were responsible for making good branch losses, supporting the proposition.
links.quote: My understanding was that they were responsible for making good losses in their branches

links.rationale: States subpostmasters were liable for losses as set out in their contracts.
links.quote: Sub-postmasters were liable for losses arising from their own carelessness, negligence or error, or for the same by their assistants, as set out in the relevant contracts.

links.rationale: Background about the Horizon contract under PFI, unrelated to loss responsibility.
links.quote: one of the first Information Technology contracts awarded under the Private Finance Initiative

links.rationale: Concerns disclosure search terms, irrelevant to the proposition.
links.quote: The search terms are generally shared with POL

links.rationale: States applicants would be responsible for losses arising in Post Office cash.
links.quote: The Applicant would be responsible for the actions of his staff, including errors they made whilst undertaking Post Office business and any loss arising in Post Office cash

links.rationale: States subpostmaster was responsible for shortfalls or losses caused by negligence, carelessness or error.
links.quote: the subpostmaster was responsible for shortfalls or losses incurred at the branch caused through his/her negligence, carelessness or error

links.rationale: Contrasts clerks with subpostmasters, affirming subpostmasters' responsibility for losses.
links.quote: that counter clerks were not responsible for making good the losses whereas Subpostmasters were.

links.rationale: Confirms agency branches (subpostmasters) were responsible for making good losses.
links.quote: agency branches were responsible for making good losses. This principle was not changed by the Impact Programme

links.rationale: States witness had no advisory role, unrelated to loss responsibility.
links.quote: I was not responsible for providing sub-postmasters / branches with advice or assistance.

links.rationale: Concerns Contract Advisors' appointment role, not responsibility for losses.
links.quote: Contract Advisors were responsible for appointing subpostmasters

links.rationale: Concerns appeal rights on termination, not responsibility for losses.
links.quote: the subpostmaster had the right to appeal this decision

links.rationale: Concerns appeal rights on termination, not responsibility for losses.
links.quote: All subpostmasters whose contracts were terminated either with or without notice had the opportunity to appeal

links.rationale: Concerns suspension authority, not contractual responsibility for losses.
links.quote: Retail Network Managers are responsible for the decision to suspend Subpostmasters

links.rationale: Concerns disciplinary roles, not loss responsibility.
links.quote: I had no role in Disciplinary matters

links.rationale: Describes retraining following losses, not contractual responsibility for them.
links.quote: I would go out to branches following losses and retrain Subpostmasters

links.rationale: Concerns who decides termination after a shortfall, not loss responsibility.
links.quote: the Contracts Advisor was responsible for deciding whether to terminate a Sub -postmasters contract.

links.rationale: Discusses holding larger losses in suspense account, not responsibility for them.
links.quote: Subpostmasters could hold them in the suspense account if permission was given

links.rationale: Describes investigative interviews about losses but does not address contractual responsibility directly.
links.quote: As a Contracts Manager I would interview Subpostmasters regarding losses following an audit.

links.rationale: Addresses Pathway's responsibility for software, a different contract matter.
links.quote: Within the contract terms Pathway were responsible for the software.

links.rationale: Describes exceptions where losses could be written off, qualifying but not negating responsibility.
links.quote: Retail Network Managers could write off losses for new Subpostmasters or if they had financial hardship

links.rationale: Describes write-off exception for new subpostmasters, qualifying default responsibility.
links.quote: New subpostmasters... were able to have losses written off during their first six weeks of trading.

proposition.text: It is denied that the Defendant failed to investigate reported discrepancies.
proposition.quote: It is denied that the Defendant failed to investigate reported discrepancies

links.rationale: Shows discrepancies were checked and reported to an investigation team, supporting that they were investigated.
links.quote: a check of Maureen McKelvey's Dockets revealed discrepancies, which in turn would have been reported to the Security and Investigation Team

links.rationale: States a team would conduct an investigation into challenged discrepancies, supporting the denial.
links.quote: would conduct an investigation and where appropriate issue an error notice, amending the initial discrepancy in whole or part

links.rationale: Expressly describes investigating and resolving identified discrepancies, supporting the denial.
links.quote: reconcile, investigate and resolve discrepancies identified against cash remittances from cash centres to branches

evidence_source.doc_id: WITN04790100 - Philip Boardman - First Witness Statement

links.rationale: An interview under caution shows the Defendant investigated reported discrepancies including alleged inflation of cash on hand.
links.quote: Mr. Ishaq denied stealing any money belonging to Post Office Ltd and also denied inflating his cash on hand to cover discrepancies in the accounts

links.rationale: A further interview putting discrepancy-related points demonstrates active investigation.
links.quote: It was put to Mr. Ishaq that his assistant, whom he had previously referred to, had stated that he had never been informed of any discrepancies

links.rationale: Describes a process for reporting discrepancies up the chain, supporting that they were acted upon.
links.quote: Any discrepancies over £1000 or an amount when theft was admitted was reported over the phone to the Contract Manager

links.rationale: Shows complaints were logged and passed to a responsible person to investigate, supporting the denial.
links.quote: complaints were also logged at the HSD, and passed to the SDM responsible for the service to investigate

links.rationale: States auditors had no responsibility to investigate the cause of discrepancies, undermining the denial.
links.quote: It was not the responsibility of the auditors to investigate the cause of the discrepancy

links.rationale: Indicates audits/investigations would be refused in certain circumstances, undermining the denial.
links.quote: Audits would be denied if: • They were requested by an agent. • There was a lack of evidence to suggest a potential shortage.

links.rationale: States the investigation was not conducted in detail, supporting a failure to investigate.
links.quote: They had some limited role in the investigation but did not investigate the case in any detail.

links.rationale: A lack of recollection of further reported discrepancies neither proves nor disproves investigation.
links.quote: I also cannot recall any further discrepancies reported by Ruth Simpson, Dorothy Day or Greg Booth.

links.rationale: Notes reported system-caused discrepancies were escalated but witness does not recall detail, so it is largely on-topic without proving investigation.
links.quote: I was aware that it had been reported by SPMs that the system could cause branch discrepancies, however I do not recall these in detail

links.rationale: Concerns a dispute over alleged theft, not whether discrepancies were investigated.
links.quote: throughout this whole episode she insisted that someone was taking the money, which was categorically denied by us all.

links.rationale: Suggests an investigatory report was inadequate and failed to reach conclusions, mildly undermining the denial.
links.quote: I think that Deloitte's report was far too caveated and failed to reach any definitive conclusions on the reliability of the Horizon IT System

links.rationale: Concerns resourcing of the SSC, not directly whether reported discrepancies were investigated.
links.quote: I do not recall any occasions where I denied requests for additional resources

links.rationale: Describes reviewing an expert's conduct rather than investigating reported discrepancies.
links.quote: I would review Gareth Jenkin's work to identify whether, and if so to what extent, he had failed in his duties as an expert witness

links.rationale: Concerns a denied allegation about removed documents, not investigation of discrepancies.
links.quote: This was strenuously denied by POL

links.rationale: Concerns issuing a recovery claim, not investigation of discrepancies.
links.quote: POL issued a claim in the Scarborough County Court seeking monies received by Mr Castleton as its agent but for which he failed to account

links.rationale: Concerns a denied request to work manually, not the investigation of discrepancies.
links.quote: Catherine agreed to this but later denied it under oath

links.rationale: About requesting reports on not guilty pleas, not discrepancy investigation.
links.quote: the approach taken by Cartwright King had been to request a report if a defendant entered a not guilty plea

links.rationale: Relates to a pleading denial about Fujitsu data editing, not investigation of discrepancies.
links.quote: it is denied that Fujitsu edited or deleted specific items of transaction data

links.rationale: Concerns appointment of an expert, unrelated to investigating discrepancies.
links.quote: The defendant and Post Office had aged t i intly a.ppoin. tarn rxpert

evidence_source.doc_id: WITN05290200 - David Smith - Second Witness Statement

links.rationale: About defendant wellbeing in litigation, not discrepancy investigation.
links.quote: A Defendant's wellbeing was not considered by POL as relevant to the manner in which litigation was conducted

links.rationale: A server failure incident is unrelated to investigating reported discrepancies.
links.quote: on the morning of 9 May 2016, the primary server failed, and a reboot of the primary server was required

links.rationale: About legal representation at interviews, not discrepancy investigation.
links.quote: the accused had been denied legal representation at them

proposition.text: The Defendant investigated complaints appropriately through its helpline and audit processes.
proposition.quote: the Defendant avers that it investigated complaints appropriately through its helpline and audit processes

links.rationale: Indicates a complaint file showed no investigation of Horizon faults, undermining claim of appropriate investigation.
links.quote: No indication in file that Horizon was investigated for faults / errors

links.rationale: Shows POL set up governance and appointed Second Sight to address Horizon complaints.
links.quote: appointing Second Sight; creating appropriate governance processes and structures

links.rationale: Concerns media contact via press office, not investigation of complaints.
links.quote: We were contacted by Panorama about its intention to cover the complaints

links.rationale: Describes structured inquiry processes drawing on complaints and audits.
links.quote: Cases for inquiry could be raised from many sources: client complaints, customer complaints, staff information, random checks, audit activity.

links.rationale: Describes helpline use for passwords, not complaint investigation.
links.quote: The POL helpline would be contacted for a one-shot password

links.rationale: Indicates audits lacked any mechanism for postmasters to raise concerns, undermining appropriate process.
links.quote: I do not recall any processes in place that allowed the SPM to raise issues or concerns during an audit.

links.rationale: Indicates a complaint was investigated by a POL employee without independence, undermining appropriateness.
links.quote: These matters were investigated by a POL employee, but not independently.

links.rationale: States POL spent considerable time investigating complaints and referred criminal cases to CCRC.
links.quote: POL spent a great deal of time investigating non-criminal complaints relating to Legacy Horizon

links.rationale: Witness understood POL acted appropriately and in good faith on advice.
links.quote: I always understood that POL was acting appropriately and in good faith

links.rationale: Shows audit team conducted review to improve processes and prevent recurrence.
links.quote: led on a review of the processes carried out by the audit team

links.rationale: Shows processes were externally audited and passed with no significant comments.
links.quote: The SSC passed this audit with no significant comments.

links.rationale: States Fujitsu had no role in audit processes but doesn't address appropriateness of investigations.
links.quote: Fujitsu had no input to POL audit processes whatsoever.

links.rationale: Suggests reports shielded under privilege as an instruction, undermining transparent investigation.
links.quote: We were asked to perform our work under legal privilege and to mark our reports appropriately; that was an instruction

links.rationale: Describes auditor training, not the appropriateness of complaint investigation.
links.quote: My audit training was through shadowing experienced auditors

links.rationale: Shows audit processes were established across the network to ensure compliance.
links.quote: I was responsible for delivering audit processes which were in place to ensure business assets and monitor compliance

links.rationale: Witness disclaims knowledge of later audit processes, providing no probative value.
links.quote: I cannot comment on audit process beyond my time as Audit Manager (2006).

links.rationale: Concerns joint expert appointment in litigation, not complaint investigation.
links.quote: The defendant and Post Office had aged t i intly a.ppoin. tarn rxpert

evidence_source.doc_id: WITN05290200 - David Smith - Second Witness Statement

links.rationale: Witness simply never used the helpline, providing no evidence on its investigative use.
links.quote: I do not recall ever having the need to call this helpline.

links.rationale: Notes consistency of audit processes but doesn't address appropriateness of complaint investigation.
links.quote: I do not recall any variation between audit processes

links.rationale: Addresses litigation conduct regarding wellbeing, not investigation processes.
links.quote: A Defendant's wellbeing was not considered by POL as relevant

links.rationale: Notes that complaints could include accounting discrepancies but doesn't address investigation quality.
links.quote: but occasionally did include complaints about branch specific accounting discrepancies

links.rationale: Witness disclaims involvement in investigating complaints, so offers no view on appropriateness.
links.quote: my involvement in the Scheme did not include either investigating complaints or checking the work

links.rationale: Describes post-settlement overhaul of audit processes without directly assessing prior appropriateness.
links.quote: there was a complete review of the Audit Process Manual to reflect the new audit processes

links.rationale: Reports findings of an investigation but doesn't establish overall appropriateness of process.
links.quote: the two material accounts we investigated had no balances over 6 months old.

links.rationale: Describes later reform to suspension/investigation approach, implying prior approach but not directly probative.
links.quote: changed the approach of suspending a postmaster and then investigating

proposition.text: It is denied that the Claimants suffered the loss and damage alleged, or any loss and damage.
proposition.quote: It is denied that the Claimants suffered the loss and damage alleged, or any loss and damage

links.rationale: Relates to the HNG-X project's internal P&L impact, not the loss allegedly suffered by claimants.
links.quote: having a significant impact on the project and lifetime profit and loss

links.rationale: Describes the witness's organisational responsibilities, not whether claimants suffered loss.
links.quote: I was responsible for driving and overseeing both of these areas.

links.rationale: Procedural detail about a report, not relevant to claimants' alleged loss.
links.quote: The Crown Office Loss and Gain report was provided to the Security team

links.rationale: Describes investigation trigger thresholds, not whether claimants suffered loss.
links.quote: they were based on loss and not any potential criminal charges

links.rationale: Describes Crown Office escalation thresholds, not the claimants' alleged loss.
links.quote: a loss in excess of £1,000, this would be escalated to the Crown Area Manager

links.rationale: Describes the investigation process for a discrepancy, not whether loss was suffered.
links.quote: my role as a contracts Manager was to investigate the loss

links.rationale: Concerns investigation triggers, not whether claimants suffered loss.
links.quote: the trigger for commencing an investigation was an `unexplained loss'

links.rationale: Concerns the investigation trigger threshold, not whether loss was suffered.
links.quote: the level of loss that would trigger an investigation was raised

links.rationale: Concerns a documentation removal allegation, not whether claimants suffered loss.
links.quote: This was strenuously denied by POL

links.rationale: Describes the suspense account procedure, not whether claimants suffered loss.
links.quote: the loss could be held in the suspense account for up to eight weeks

links.rationale: Concerns who bears the burden of proving a loss, not whether the claimants actually suffered loss.
links.quote: The onus was on the Subpostmaster to prove the loss

links.rationale: Concerns Crown Office (employee-run) shortfalls, not the subpostmaster claimants' alleged loss.
links.quote: In Crown Offices (owned and managed by Post Office employees) any resulting shortfall in cash was declared as a loss.

links.rationale: States a contractual liability term, not whether claimants actually suffered loss.
links.quote: any loss arising in Post Office cash

links.rationale: Shows subpostmasters had to personally make good losses, indicating they did bear financial loss.
links.quote: When there was a loss, it was for the SPM to make that loss good.

links.rationale: Shows losses had to be made good by the subpostmaster, indicating financial loss was borne.
links.quote: Any loss had to be made good either by cheque or cash

links.rationale: Shows the claimant had a loss he had to make good, indicating he bore the financial loss.
links.quote: When Mr Castleton confirmed that he was able to make the loss good

links.rationale: A claimant describes being made to write off unresolved losses, supporting that loss was suffered.
links.quote: we were advised to write off the loss as a "discrepancy"

links.rationale: Shows subpostmasters had to make good losses, indicating they bore the financial loss.
links.quote: otherwise the Subpostmaster would have to make good the loss

links.rationale: Indicates a real loss existed at a claimant's branch which he had to address, undercutting the denial that loss was suffered.
links.quote: alleging that substantially all loss was created by Horizon

links.rationale: Describes altering cash declarations to keep operating, ambiguous as to actual loss suffered by claimant.
links.quote: She denied stealing any money but explained that she had altered the cash declarations in order to continue to operate the branch

links.rationale: Suggests an alleged shortfall may have been caused by a system error, supporting that the claimant bore an unwarranted loss.
links.quote: whether a system error was causing the alleged shortfall

links.rationale: Acknowledges losses commonly arose at branches due to error, supporting that losses occurred.
links.quote: it was common for branches to have a loss of this amount due to an error

links.rationale: Confirms a real loss appeared in cash declarations and at audit at a claimant's branch.
links.quote: previous cash declarations had shown an initial loss which was similar to the final loss at audit

links.rationale: Confirms an unusually high level of loss at a claimant's branch.
links.quote: there were no other offices that I dealt with which were experiencing this level of loss.

links.rationale: Frames losses as concealed by subpostmasters' false accounting, ambiguous on whether claimants suffered genuine loss.
links.quote: had inflated their cash on hand figures to cover up a loss

proposition.text: The contractual allocation of responsibility did not extend to losses caused by the Defendant's own system.
proposition.responds_to: Defence averment of contractual responsibility for losses (para 15)
proposition.quote: deny that the contractual allocation of responsibility extended to losses caused by the Defendant's own system

links.rationale: Concerns a role limited to sales and unrelated to contractual loss allocation.
links.quote: In March 2005 my area was extended again but limited to the responsibility for sales and no contractual issues.

links.rationale: Concerns extending the Fujitsu contract, unrelated to the subpostmaster loss allocation.
links.quote: The business case was to extend the c ntract with Fuj,tsL on r ew terms until i° arci, 2015 and to replace the existing system

evidence_source.doc_id: WITN05290200 - David Smith - Second Witness Statement

links.rationale: Concerns a disclosure decision causing loss of opportunity, irrelevant to contractual loss allocation.
links.quote: our decision concerning family review - for which I take full responsibility -- caused the loss of an opportunity to identify Appendix 6

links.rationale: Concerns chasing action points, unrelated to contractual loss allocation.
links.quote: I took responsibility for trying to resolve some action points that were raised

links.rationale: Concerns when a witness became aware of Horizon, irrelevant to loss allocation.
links.quote: During 2009, I became aware of the Horizon IT system by virtue of my involvement in relevant contractual work

links.rationale: Concerns the scope of a witness's work, not contractual loss allocation.
links.quote: My work did not extend to the original or older Horizon system (pre-2010), also referred to as 'Legacy Horizon'

links.rationale: Limits contractual responsibility to losses caused by the subpostmaster's own negligence, carelessness or error, supporting that it did not extend to system-caused losses.
links.quote: From a purely contractual perspective a sub postmaster or other agent is responsible for all losses caused through his own negligence, carelessness or error.

links.rationale: Frames the contractual obligation as confined to negligence/error and assistants' losses, not losses from the Defendant's system.
links.quote: The Contract provided that he was obliged to make good all losses caused through his own negligence, carelessness or error and losses of any kind caused by his assistants.

links.rationale: Restricts responsibility to shortfalls caused by negligence/carelessness/error, supporting that system-caused losses fall outside the contract.
links.quote: the subpostmaster was responsible for shortfalls or losses incurred at the branch caused through his/her negligence, carelessness or error

links.rationale: Confines liability to the subpostmaster's own carelessness, negligence or error, consistent with the denial.
links.quote: Sub-postmasters were liable for losses arising from their own carelessness, negligence or error, or for the same by their assistants, as set out in the relevant contracts.

links.rationale: Defines responsibility by reference to assistants' conduct, consistent with the contract not covering system-caused losses.
links.quote: The subpostmaster was also responsible for losses of all kinds caused by their assistants working in the branch

links.rationale: Addresses disclosure of defect documents, not the contractual allocation of system-caused losses.
links.quote:
Had we ever received documents that showed that defects in the Horizon IT system at the Marine Drive branch caused illusory losses during the period in
question, we would have disclosed them.

links.rationale: Concerns belief in Horizon's robustness, not the contractual scope of loss responsibility.
links.quote: At no time did I ever get the impression that for one minute did Ms Talbot believe that the Horizon IT system caused losses at the Marine Drive branch.

links.rationale: References general complaints about the contractual recovery position but provides no detail on system-caused losses allocation.
links.quote: there was a regular background level of general complaint from the NFSP about crime, losses and the POL contractual position on recoveries

links.rationale: States POL bore responsibility for its own system, supporting that system-related responsibility did not rest with the subpostmaster.
links.quote: The responsibility for monitoring the Horizon IT System ("Horizon") lay with POL

links.rationale: States responsibility extended to making good any losses incurred, contradicting the claim that system-caused losses were excluded.
links.quote: they were responsible for making good any losses incurred.

links.rationale: Concerns which team would investigate Horizon issues, not contractual loss allocation.
links.quote: NBSC would not have been able to identify if there were any issues caused by the Horizon system

links.rationale: Concerns impressions about Horizon's operation, not the contractual scope of responsibility.
links.quote: At no stage did I ever get any impression from POL or Fujitsu that caused me to believe that they thought there was anything wrong with the operation of the Horizon system

links.rationale: Concerns absence of reports of system-caused discrepancies, not contractual allocation.
links.quote: I have no recollection of any other Post Office branches reporting discrepancies caused by the Horizon system at this time

links.rationale: Notes an allegation of Horizon error in a case but does not address contractual loss allocation.
links.quote: the functionality of the Horizon System would be an issue

links.rationale: Records a claim that Horizon caused losses but does not address how the contract allocated responsibility.
links.quote: Mrs Wolstenholme claimed that the Horizon system itself caused losses in the sub post office accounts.

links.rationale: Concerns whether Horizon caused losses factually, not the contractual allocation of responsibility.
links.quote: she had been unable to identify any basis upon which the Horizon system could have caused the losses at the Marine Drive branch.

links.rationale: Addresses lack of information about bugs, not how the contract allocated responsibility for system losses.
links.quote: I do not recollect being informed by POL or Fujitsu of such issues.

links.rationale: Asserts the system was not shown to cause losses but does not speak to the contractual scope of responsibility.
links.quote: in none of the cases [in the Scheme] has our computer system been shown to have caused the losses complained of

links.rationale: Addresses system development responsibility between POCL and Pathway, not the subpostmaster loss allocation.
links.quote: It was Pathway and not POCL's responsibility to develop their system in accordance with the requirements of private finance.
